column1: # mapping_set_title: 'OCSF to NIST NVD class mappings'
#   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   classes and the NIST National Vulnerability Database (NVD) LinkML schema.
#   NVD enriches CVE records with CVSS scores, CPE applicability, and weakness
#   attributions. OCSF Cvss maps directly to NVD CVSSMetric; vulnerability and
#   affected-package objects correspond to NVD enrichment fields.

column8: OCSF Cvss and NVD CVSSMetric both capture CVSS version, vector string, and base/temporal/environmental scores for a vulnerability.
OCSF Cve models the CVE Program record; NVD NVDEntry is the NVD-enriched view of the same CVE with additional CVSS, CPE, and CWE data.
OCSF Vulnerability wraps CVE references and CVSS details closely aligned to the NVD enrichment model; close match because OCSF may reference non-NVD sources.
OCSF AffectedPackage carries vendor/product/version/CPE data; NVD CPEMatch encodes the same data in CPE applicability statements — structurally equivalent with minor granularity differences.
EPSS scores contextualise NVD vulnerability data; NVD NVDEntry includes an EPSS indicator where available — related via shared vulnerability record.
A VulnerabilityFinding event surfaces NVD-sourced data (CVSS, CPE, CWE) for a detected CVE; structurally close to an NVD entry.
OCSF Cwe carries a CWE identifier and caption; NVD NVDWeakness stores the weakness attribution row associated with an NVD entry — same concept, different container.
